lead: A moving day in Beccles has its own particular shape, set partly by the town's narrow Georgian streets and partly by the river that runs through the heart of it. After many years carrying homes into Beccles from our Westerfield base, we have learned where a day can stall and how to keep it flowing smoothly. This checklist walks you through the run-up, the morning itself and your first hours in the new home, with the local detail that turns a Beccles move from a scramble into a calm and well-managed day.

sections.h: Two Weeks Out: Plan Your Access
sections.body:
In Beccles, the first thing to sort is not packing but access, and that is especially true if your new home sits in the old town. The historic streets that climb up from the Quay are narrow, some are tight enough that a long removal lorry cannot easily stop, and parking near the centre can be restricted. Work out early where a vehicle can realistically pull up, and tell us as soon as you can. With that knowledge we can plan properly, perhaps shuttling your belongings the final stretch with a smaller van, or timing the unload to avoid the busiest part of the day.

If your new place is on one of the newer developments around the edge of Beccles, the day is far simpler, with driveways and room to manoeuvre, though it is always worth checking for any temporary restrictions if building work is going on nearby. Either way, this fortnight is for confirming your moving date with us, running down the freezer, notifying the important people of your change of address and arranging mail redirection. If your chain is looking shaky, talk to us now about storage rather than leaving it to the last minute.

Gather your packing materials this week too, or ask us to supply them. Good strong boxes, plenty of tape, bubble wrap for the fragile things and marker pens for labelling all make a real difference, and running out halfway through is a guaranteed source of stress. Having everything you need in the house before you begin means the packing flows steadily rather than stalling each time you reach for materials that simply are not there.

sections.h: One Week Before: Pack Room by Room
sections.body:
With a week to go, packing should be well under way. Work methodically through the house one room at a time rather than darting about, and begin with the spaces you use least: the spare room, the loft, the garage and the shed. The loft is the classic trap, holding far more than anyone remembers, and discovering heavy boxes up a ladder on moving morning throws the whole timetable out, which matters all the more when you have a tight unloading window arranged in central Beccles.

Label every box clearly on the side as well as the top, marking which room it belongs to in your new Beccles home along with a note of the contents. As we unload, we carry boxes straight to the right room, so clear labels stop your belongings piling up in the hall. Colour coding works beautifully here: a colour for each room, matching stickers on the boxes and a matching sheet on each door at the new house, so even a crew seeing the property for the first time can unload at speed, which is just what you want when time on a narrow street is limited.

Set aside anything precious, important documents, jewellery and small valuables, to travel in your own car rather than the lorry. Mark fragile boxes on more than one side, and keep a simple numbered inventory if any part of your move involves storage or a staggered handover. By the end of this week, the only things left to pack should be the kettle, the breakfast things and the beds you are sleeping in the night before the move.

sections.h: The Day Before: Tie Up the Loose Ends
sections.body:
The day before a Beccles move is for finishing everything off. Defrost and empty the fridge and freezer, complete the last of the packing, and strip all but tonight's beds, so that when we arrive in the morning everything is sealed and ready except the kettle and the breakfast things. The single biggest cause of a slow, stressful moving day is unfinished packing when the crew turns up, and in Beccles that delay can eat straight into a carefully arranged parking or access window, so being genuinely ready matters here more than in most places.

Pack your essentials box, the most important box of all, and keep it for your own car rather than the lorry. Into it goes the kettle, mugs, tea, milk and biscuits, phone chargers, basic toiletries, a change of clothes, any medication, important documents and a few toys for the children. This is the box that keeps everyone going, letting you make a brew the moment you arrive without opening the whole van. Take and note your meter readings at the old property, and charge your phone overnight so it is ready for the day.

Give a thought to the morning route as well. The centre of Beccles can be busy, the streets near the river are narrow, and market days bring extra bustle that can catch out anyone in a hurry. If your move involves driving through or near the town centre, a little timing thought tonight saves frustration tomorrow. Clear a path from the front door to the kerb at both properties, and if you have children or pets, arrange care for the day or set up one cleared room as their safe base.

sections.h: Moving Morning: Making the Window Count
sections.body:
On the morning itself, being ready and being present is everything, and never more so than in a town where unloading time can be tight. Open the curtains, switch on the lights so we can see clearly, and make sure nothing is still hidden in the loft, shed or behind a door. When we arrive we like a quick walk-round so you can point out the fragile boxes, the antiques and anything needing extra care, particularly if you are moving period furniture into or out of an older Beccles home with narrow stairs and low doorways.

Leave out a kettle, mugs and a few biscuits for the team if you can; it keeps everyone going and it is the small kindness our crews always appreciate. Keep children and pets in their safe room with a clear note on the door, because the front door will stand open for much of the morning and cats in particular will bolt given half a chance. Tell us where they are so we all keep that door shut. If parking near your Beccles home is restricted, let us get straight to loading so we make full use of the available window.

Before we leave the old property, do a proper walk-through together. Check every cupboard, the loft, the shed, the garden and behind every door, because things always get left in the rush. Confirm the inventory, sort the handover of the old keys and make sure you have the keys for your new Beccles home. Then meet us at the other end, where we will unload to the right rooms and rebuild the beds so you have somewhere comfortable to sleep that first night.

sections.h: Arriving in Beccles: The First Few Hours
sections.body:
Arriving at the new home is where all that careful labelling pays off. As we unload, we carry each box to the room marked on its side, so even with a tight unloading window your home quickly takes shape rather than becoming one great heap in the hall. Point us to where the big furniture should go before we bring it in; placing a wardrobe correctly first time is far easier than shuffling it once it is full, and in older Beccles properties with awkward corners that planning really counts. If you have colour-coded the doors, the whole unload moves faster and with fewer questions.

Get the kitchen working enough for a brew and a simple meal, and ask us to make up the beds before we leave. After a long day, the difference between a made-up bed and a mattress buried under boxes is the difference between a good first night in Beccles and a miserable one. Find your essentials box, put the kettle on, and resist the temptation to unpack everything tonight. The boxes will still be there in the morning, and a proper rest is the best possible start in your new town.

Over the following days, unpack at a sensible pace, room by room, and take time to enjoy where you now live. A first stroll along the Quay, a look at the boats on the Waveney, a wander through the Georgian streets and a browse around the market are how a new house starts to feel like home. Register with a local GP and dentist, sort your utility and council accounts, and let yourself settle in gently. We will have done our part by then, and every load we carry is fully covered, so you can put your energy into enjoying life in Beccles.

faqs: What should I sort out first before moving day in Beccles?

Access comes before anything else. The historic streets climbing up from the Quay are narrow with restricted parking, so work out where a lorry can stop and tell us early. We can use a smaller shuttle van or time the unload to suit. Newer developments on the edge of Beccles are far simpler.
Why does being fully packed matter so much for a Beccles move?

Because unloading time in the old town is often limited by narrow streets and parking restrictions. Unfinished packing when the crew arrives eats into that window. Being sealed, labelled and ready, with only the kettle and beds left, lets us load straight away and keep to the agreed plan.
What should go in my essentials box for a Beccles move?

Pack the kettle, mugs, tea, milk and biscuits, phone chargers, basic toiletries, a change of clothes, any medication, important documents and a few toys for children. Keep it in your own car so it is to hand the moment you reach your new Beccles home and can make a brew straight away.
Can you move period furniture into an older Beccles home?

Yes. We regularly carry large and period pieces into Beccles homes with narrow stairs, tight hallways and low doorways. We plan those challenges in advance, measure where needed and take real care, so your furniture arrives safely. Every load we move is fully covered for your peace of mind.
